item.title: UGC chief urges alignment of HEAT project with govt priorities
item.description: The University Grants Commission of Bangladesh has asked stakeholders to revise the Development Project Proposal of the Higher Education Acceleration and Transformation project by June, aligning it with national priorities and sectoral needs....Geographic Reference

item.title: Factory inspectors need training, logistics: Ariful
item.description: Labour and employment minister Ariful Haque Choudhury on Tuesday stressed the need for improved training, logistical support and security for factory inspectors to strengthen workplace monitoring and safeguard workers’ rights....

item.title: BITAC holds certificate ceremony, job fair
item.description: The Bangladesh Industrial Technical Assistance Centre organised a certificate distribution ceremony and job fair for trainees under the Skills for Industry Competitiveness and Innovation Programme in Dhaka on April 22....

item.title: NUS calls for urgent action against violence, rape
item.description: Speakers at a discussion meeting in the capital on Tuesday called for urgent and coordinated action to ensure justice for violence against women and children, including rape, and to end what they described as a growing culture of impunity....

item.title: NSU holds creative writing course closing ceremony
item.description: North South University’s department of English and modern languages hosted the closing ceremony for its inaugural Certificate Course in Creative Writing on Saturday....Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: 16 DU students receive Tk 2.5 lakh in scholarships
item.description: Sixteen students of University of Dhaka received scholarships worth around Tk 2.5 lakh under two memorial trust funds at a ceremony held on Tuesday....

item.title: Shanto-Mariam University admission fair begins
item.description: The admission fair at Shanto-Mariam University of Creative Technology commenced with a vibrant opening ceremony marked by an interactive guidance session on Tuesday....

item.title: DU students form human chain demanding justice for Mimo
item.description: Students of Dhaka University’s Theatre and Performance Studies department formed a human chain on Tuesday demanding justice and punishment for those involved in the death of their fellow student Munira Mahjabin Mimo....

item.title: Israel issues new evacuation warning for south Lebanon
item.description: Israel on Tuesday said it was not seeking to take territory in Lebanon, as its military issued a wave of new evacuation warnings for towns and villages in the battle-scarred south....

item.title: IUB workshop highlights mushroom farming challenges
item.description: A workshop on modern mushroom cultivation techniques at Independent University, Bangladesh engaged 20 entrepreneurs and farmers on Monday, focusing on production challenges and market access....

item.title: Info adviser admits militancy exists, home minister denies
item.description: Prime minister’s information adviser Zahed Ur Rahman on Tuesday said that militancy existed in Bangladesh while home minister Salahuddin Ahmed denied the presence of any such activity amid heightened police alerts over possible attacks on key installations......

item.title: UK to work with Bangladesh on stolen asset recovery: envoy
item.description: British high commissioner to Bangladesh Sarah Cooke on Tuesday said that the United Kingdom was closely working with Bangladesh for recovery of assets acquired by Bangladeshi individuals using funds misappropriated at home in the past....

item.title: Crops of two Sunamganj haors go under water
item.description: The boro crops of two haors at two upazilas in Sunamganj went under water in the early morning on Tuesday due to incessant heavy rainfall since Sunday and flood water flowing down a cross border river....

item.title: Heavy rain triggers severe water stagnation in Ctg city
item.description: Heavy rain on Tuesday caused severe water stagnation in different areas of Chattogram city, submerging many roads under waist-to-chest-deep water and causing immense suffering to city dwellers....

item.title: MPs debate national consensus, referendum
item.description: The Ruling and opposition lawmakers engaged in a heated debate in Jatiya Sangsad on Tuesday over the national consensus on the July National Charter and the legitimacy of referendum questions....

item.title: Suicide among police members rising
item.description: Suicide among lower-level police personnel is rising mainly due to additional working hours, poor living conditions, financial hardship and an imbalance between professional and personal life....

item.title: Peace efforts stall, US examines Iran proposal
item.description: Efforts to end the Middle East war appeared at a standstill on Tuesday, with the US considering Tehran’s latest offer to unblock the Strait of Hormuz, and Iran saying Washington could no longer dictate terms....

item.title: More for govt to do to curb death
item.description: BENGAL nor’westers, flashes of lightning and thunderous clouds have coexisted with the people of this land for centuries. Yet, a pressing question has now emerged: when and how did this natural phenomenon turned into a silent death trap? Why has lightning, once a fleeting fear, now become a symbol of inevitable mortality for rural people? April this year has not......

item.title: Beijing’s calculated patience in Middle East
item.description: THE current volatility in the Middle East has once again placed the global security architecture under immense strain. Despite a fragile ceasefire, the shipping lanes of the Strait of Hormuz remain a graveyard of commercial activity. As of late April 2026, vessel tracking data shows a near-standstill, with fewer than five ships transiting the waterway daily. With diplomatic trust......

item.title: Rising cost of education in Dhaka
item.description: THE rising cost of education in Dhaka has reached a point where it can no longer be treated as a routine concern. For a growing number of families, it has become a persistent and often overwhelming financial burden. Education, which ought to function as a basic right and a pathway to social mobility, is increasingly experienced as an expensive service. Even for......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Implications of fuel price increase
item.description: THE recent surge in fuel prices has become a critical economic and social issue, with far-reaching implications for inflation, production costs, income distribution and macroeconomic stability. As a net importer of energy, Bangladesh remains highly vulnerable to global oil market fluctuations. Domestic fuel price adjustments, often influenced by international......

item.title: Unequal trade deal with US must be revisited
item.description: THE interim government in its final week signed a trade agreement with the United States that manypoliticaleconomists called outrightly asymmetrical. The agreement on reciprocal trade between Bangladesh and the United States, signed on February 10, was framed by the interim government as a breakthrough to avert US punitive tariffs. The deal locks Dhaka into directed......Geographic Reference

item.title: Govt should aim for progressive tax reform
item.description: THE proposed revenue target of Tk 6 lakh crore or more in the budget for the 2026–27 financial year, with about two-thirds expected to come from value-added tax and other indirect levies, highlights a troubling continuity in fiscal policy. Despite repeated warnings from economists, the government appears set to further its reliance on regressive, indirect taxation......

item.title: PSG edge Bayern in nine-goal Champions League semi-final epic
item.description:
Khvicha Kvaratskhelia and Ousmane Dembele scored twice each as Paris Saint-Germain beat Bayern Munich 5-4 in an epic first leg of their Champions League semi-final on Tuesday, an extraordinary encounter becoming the highest-scoring match ever at this stage of the competition.
...Breaking news alerts

item.title: Second phase of West Bengal election today
item.description: Voting for the West Bengal Assembly elections 2026 phase 2 will take place today, covering a large number of constituencies across the state, reports India Today....Sports news updates

item.title: Explosions rock Kyiv, six killed in Russia, Ukraine
item.description: Explosions echoed over Kyiv on Tuesday during a rare daytime Russian drone attack on the capital, after the two sides launched strikes that left six dead....

item.title: Govt to borrow $1.9 billion in non-concessional loans
item.description: The government on Tuesday approved five non-concessional loan proposals to borrow $1.9 billion to meet the foreign currency shortage for the payment of energy bills gone up because of supply and price shocks amid the war in the Gulf region....

item.title: EU for more women participation, transparent financing in polls
item.description: The European Union in its final report on the 13th parliamentary elections in Bangladesh on Tuesday recommended affirmative actions to enhance women participation inpoliticsand the introduction of strict campaign financing rules to hold candidates and......

item.title: Elderly Indian woman found dead in Dhaka
item.description: An Indian national on Tuesday morning was brought dead to the Continental Hospital in the capital’s Gulshan area after she had fallen down in the washroom of her Baridhara residence....Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Over 40 hurt in clashes in Sunamganj
item.description: At least forty people were injured in two separate clashes at Derai upazila in Sunamganj on Tuesday....

item.title: Man accused of killing wife in Sylhet
item.description: A man allegedly stabbed his wife to death at Kanaighat upazila in Sylhet on Tuesday early hours....

item.title: Ex-Biman MD gets bail in child house help torture case
item.description: A court in Dhaka on Tuesday granted bail to former managing director and chief executive officer of Biman Bangladesh Airlines Md Shafiqur Rahman in a case of torturing a child house help....

item.title: 3 buddhist monks killed in Ctg road accident
item.description: At least three people were killed and five others injured as a microbus lost control and fell into a roadside ditch at Lohagara in Chattogram on Tuesday morning....Politics

item.title: BCG arrests 14 over cement smuggling to Myanmar
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested 14 suspected smugglers, including an alleged syndicate leader, and seized a large consignment of cement during joint operations in coastal areas on Monday....

item.title: Govt orders probe, action over unsafe school meals
item.description: The government has ordered investigations and strict action after reports that primary school students fell ill from contaminated mid-day meals under the school feeding programme....

item.title: 2 doctors detained after child dies during surgery in Kushtia
item.description: The police detained two doctors after a six-year-old girl died during a surgical procedure at a private hospital in Kushtia on Monday night....
